```json
  "findings": [
    "Mortar joints are not tooled to a concave profile as required for weather resistance.",
    "Improper or absent concave tooling creates a direct path for water entry into the wall assembly.",
    "Water intrusion can lead to moisture damage, efflorescence, freeze-thaw deterioration, and reduced wall durability."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Tool all bed and head joints to a proper concave profile while mortar is thumbprint hard.",
    "Verify full mortar joint coverage and tooling depth per TMS 602 workmanship requirements.",
    "Inspect and repair any water-damaged materials before closing out the wall."
  ],
  "rationale": "TMS 602 requires mortar joints in unit masonry to be tooled to a concave profile to provide a water-resistant joint surface. Joints that are raked, flush, or improperly tooled compromise the wall's ability to shed water and are considered defective workmanship. Because the work is rough-complete and the defect affects weather protection, the installation fails inspection.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 602 Specification for Masonry Structures - workmanship requirements for tooling mortar joints",
    "TMS 402 Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures - durability and weather resistance provisions"
  ]
}
```
